title: Puffed Malty Jamaican Baguette Bowl
description: A malty, aerated, indulgent Jamaican dish featuring baguette, sashimi-cut ingredients, and served in a Korean brass bowl, suitable for shojin-friendly dietary preferences.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Slice the baguette into 1-inch thick rounds and toast until lightly browned.
In a blender, combine malted milk powder, scotch bonnet peppers, carrots, green onions, and melted vegan butter, and blend until smooth.
In a large bowl, whisk together soy sauce, toasted sesame seeds, and crushed kombu seaweed.
Add the blended mixture to the bowl and stir until well combined.
Add the toasted baguette rounds to the bowl and toss until they are evenly coated with the mixture.
Transfer the mixture to a Korean brass bowl and bake for 15 minutes, or until the baguette is puffed and golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve hot, garnished with additional green onions and toasted sesame seeds if desired.
